extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends around a mid-level (~0.45–0.5), with a long central high plateau (~0.8–0.85) between roughly 1/3 and 2/3 of the time.
R2: There is at least one additional short excursion (either a bump up or a dip down) outside the central plateau region.
R3: The lowest level reached anywhere stays above ~0.1 (no very deep, long-lasting trough).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single, dominant high plateau (~0.8–0.9) centered in the middle, starting after the first third and ending before the last third of the time.
R2: Outside this plateau (at beginning and end), the signal stays near a stable mid-level (~0.45–0.5) without extra pronounced bumps or dips.
R3: There are no additional large peaks or deep troughs apart from the main central rise and fall.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long central low plateau (~0.1–0.2) between roughly 1/3 and 2/3 of the time, clearly lower than the start and end levels.
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal returns to a higher mid-level (~0.45–0.5).
R3: There is at least one sharp, narrow deep dip (a “spike down”) near either the beginning or the end, going briefly below the surrounding mid-level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series stays around a mid value (~0.45–0.5) before and after the pattern.  
R2: There is one long plateau that is clearly separated from the baseline (either much higher ~0.8 or much lower ~0.1–0.2) and occupies roughly the middle half of the time axis.  
R3: There is a shorter, opposite-direction excursion (a bump if the middle is low, or a dip if the middle is high) that occurs well separated from the central plateau, near one of the edges.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series baseline is roughly constant at a mid value (~0.45–0.5) at the beginning and end.  
R2: There is a single, long, elevated plateau (~0.8–0.9) centered in the middle of the time axis.  
R3: Outside this high plateau there are no other strong excursions: no significant dips below baseline and no additional peaks comparable to the main plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series baseline is roughly constant at a mid value (~0.45–0.5) at the beginning and end.  
R2: There is one long, central low plateau (~0.1–0.2) in the middle of the time axis.  
R3: In addition to the central low plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, deep dip below the baseline located near one or both edges of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the series.  
R2: There is a long plateau above 0.75 roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: There is an additional shorter excursion in the opposite direction (either an early high spike before the plateau, or a late high spike after a central dip), so the series shows both a long plateau and a separate shorter opposite excursion.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 at the beginning and end of the series.  
R2: There is a single long plateau above 0.75 starting near index 50 and ending near index 100.  
R3: Outside that single high plateau, the signal stays close to the baseline without any large additional peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the series.  
R2: There is a long central valley below 0.25 starting near index 40 and ending near index 100.  
R3: There is at least one additional short, sharp dip below 0.25 outside this central valley (near the beginning or near the end).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau clearly above 0.7 roughly in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this central high plateau, the series stays near 0.45–0.55 most of the time.  
R3: There is at most one additional short excursion: either a brief high peak above 0.7 near one edge, or a brief low dip below 0.3 near one edge.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau above 0.75 located centrally (no additional high or low blocks).  
R2: Before and after this plateau, values stay in a narrow band around 0.45–0.55 without other pronounced peaks or dips.  
R3: The minimum value of the series remains above 0.4 (no deep dips).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau below 0.25 in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: On both sides of this low plateau, the values stay around 0.45–0.5 for extended periods.  
R3: At least one of the edges (early or late in time) contains a short, sharp dip well below 0.25.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long central segment where the series is far from the baseline (either clearly higher or clearly lower) for roughly one half of the time axis.
R2: On one side of this long central segment there is an additional short excursion in the opposite direction (a brief peak if the central segment is low, or a brief dip if the central segment is high).
R3: Outside these excursions, the series stays close to a flat baseline around the middle of the value range.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is a single long plateau above the baseline (series rises sharply from the baseline, stays high and flat for a long interval, then returns sharply).
R2: Before the rise and after the fall, the series stays close to a flat baseline at a mid‑level value.
R3: No extra peaks or dips occur outside the main high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a single long valley below the baseline (series drops sharply from the baseline, stays low and flat for a long interval, then returns sharply).
R2: Before the drop and after the rise, the series stays close to a flat baseline at a mid‑level value.
R3: One or two additional short, narrow spikes (brief drops or rises) appear near the ends, much shorter than the main central valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8) in the middle of the series.  
R2: Outside this plateau, the signal stays around 0.5.  
R3: There is an additional short excursion (either a peak above 0.7 or a dip below 0.3) on one side of the long plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8–0.9) in the middle of the series.  
R2: Before the plateau the signal is roughly constant around 0.48–0.5.  
R3: After the plateau the signal returns to roughly the same level as before, with no extra large peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long low-valued valley (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle of the series.  
R2: Before and after this valley, the signal is roughly constant around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: In addition to the long valley, there is at least one narrow, deeper local dip (sharp V-shape) on one side of the valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long plateau at a high value (around 0.8) in the middle of the series.  
R2: Before and/or after this long high plateau there is a short, isolated spike in the opposite direction (either a short high spike before a long low segment, or a short low “dip” before a long high segment).  
R3: The baseline at the beginning and end is around 0.45–0.5.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium baseline around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: There is a single long high plateau (around 0.8–0.9) roughly centered in the series.  
R3: Apart from the start/end baselines and this one high plateau, there are no extra spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series spends a long central portion at a low level (around 0.1–0.2).  
R2: The beginning and end of the series are at a higher baseline (around 0.45–0.5).  
R3: There are one or two short, sharp dips from this higher baseline downwards near the edges of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series baseline is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the time.
R2: There is one long plateau with values clearly above 0.75.
R3: There is exactly one short, isolated excursion (spike or dip) on the opposite side of the baseline from the long plateau (e.g., a short high spike if the long plateau is low, or a short low dip if the long plateau is high).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series baseline is around 0.45–0.5 for the beginning and end.
R2: There is one long central plateau with values above 0.75.
R3: Outside this plateau there are no other strong peaks or deep dips; the rest stays near baseline with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series baseline is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the time.
R2: There is one long central segment where the values drop clearly below 0.2.
R3: In addition to this main low segment, there is at least one extra narrow, very deep dip (sharp valley) significantly below the baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long mid‑segment (roughly between indices 40 and 110) whose values are clearly higher than the baseline (a plateau near 0.8 when the baseline is around 0.5).  
R2: One additional short excursion in the opposite direction appears near one edge: either an early high spike (around indices 20–30) when the middle is low, or an early/late low valley when the middle is high.  
R3: Outside these excursions, the beginning and end of the series stay close to the same baseline level (around 0.45–0.5).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single, long, central high plateau (approximately indices 50–100) where values are significantly above baseline (around 0.8–0.9 vs. baseline 0.47–0.5).  
R2: Before and after this central plateau, the values quickly drop and remain near a flat baseline close to 0.47–0.5, with no other large spikes or dips.  
R3: The start and end segments are roughly symmetric in shape and level around that baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long, wide central low valley (approximately indices 40–100) where values are clearly below baseline (around 0.1–0.2 vs. baseline near 0.48–0.5).  
R2: Before and after this central valley the series returns to a flat, higher baseline around 0.48–0.5.  
R3: At least one additional narrow deep dip (short in time and going even lower than the valley or baseline) appears near one edge of the series (early or late).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long plateau at a high value (around 0.8) covering roughly the central part of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this plateau, both the early and late segments stay near a medium baseline (around 0.45–0.5).  
R3: Exactly one extra, much shorter excursion (either an additional peak above the baseline or a dip below it) appears on one side of the long plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau (around 0.8–0.9) in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: The values before this plateau stay near a medium baseline (~0.48–0.5) without additional pronounced peaks or dips.  
R3: The values after this plateau also return to and stay near the same medium baseline without additional pronounced peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: Before this low plateau, values stay at a higher baseline (~0.45–0.5) and may contain a short, sharp dip.  
R3: After this low plateau, values return to a similar higher baseline and may again contain a short, sharp dip.
